design_decision: Worker-key shape: server-issued durable bind_handle + client worker_key as lookup, stored on agent_claims (+worker_key/worker_key_pool_id/bind_handle cols, partial unique index). Keyed-bound agents excluded from free pool expiry-independently (verifier fix).

phases.title: server-side stable worker binding
phases.outcome: claimAgent(pool,secret,workerKey?) — keyed binding reuses same users row + fresh token; migration 0022; identity-sharing hole closed (free-agent select excludes worker_key IS NOT NULL regardless of expiry). server 1296, full 10/10.

phases.title: MCP client persists + presents worker key (reconnect re-binds same identity)
phases.outcome: api-client claimAgent(pool,secret,workerKey?) + index.ts reads PM_WORKER_KEY; cleanup SKIPS releaseAgent when keyed (verifier fix — else release deletes the binding and respawn first-binds a new identity); bindHandle ignored; docs. mcp-server 126/126, typecheck 6/6, build 5/5. Keyless byte-identical.

phases.title: seal — shared-host distinctness + back-compat + force-claim + docs + full suite

open_issues: BLOCKER (external): the working tree carries ~13 uncommitted files implementing a parallel 'requeue' merge-request feature (audit action requeue + integrator-ref batch/loop/sse + server merge-request.service/event-bus + web). Not from this campaign (tree was clean at branch). It breaks full pnpm test (audit.test.ts exhaustive AUDIT_ACTIONS assertion has no 'requeue' case). C1 work is green in isolation. Surfaced to user 2026-06-06 — awaiting decision on reconciling the parallel WIP before C1-P3 seal + C3 (which also touch server/web/event-bus and could entangle).
next_campaign: C3 — liveness surfacing (claim_state in reads + pick-next + badges + alerts/handoff), on the same branch after C1.
